target.name: EraDrive
target.founder: Sumant Sharma, Justin Kruger, Simone D'Amico

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
EraDrive is a seed-stage autonomous systems company in the Drones & Autonomous sector, having raised $5.3M to date. The broader sector includes eight private peers (Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, Thoron among them) and no public comparables, limiting traditional peer-median valuation leverage.

Given the early stage and sparse near-peer dataset, stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable approach. Using total raised ($5.3M) as a valuation proxy and applying a standard 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-Series A progression yields $13.25M. This single-method triangulation is conservative relative to later-stage peers in the autonomous sector but appropriately reflects EraDrive's current maturity and capital efficiency.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $11M to $15M, with $13M as the median point. Key uncertainties center on product-market validation metrics not captured in this dataset—technical progress, customer pipeline, and capital efficiency relative to the broader cohort would materially inform movement within or beyond this band. The absence of tighter peer comparables at seed stage is the binding constraint; as EraDrive progresses and more stage-adjacent data emerges, peer-median methods will provide stronger anchoring power.
